target.title: Require signed beta desktop distribution
target.body:
## Summary
- Point beta and alpha desktop builds at channel-specific Sparkle appcasts.
- Make `scripts/make_appcast.sh` honor beta/alpha appcast output paths.
- Require signed beta desktop assets plus `appcast-beta.xml` in the beta verifier.
- Add `--desktop-only` and gate beta release publish before npm/plugin dispatch so beta npm cannot get ahead of signed desktop distribution.
- Repair release-branch CI guard failures surfaced after forced base updates:
  - cron tool nullable-clear affordances now stay provider-compatible with plain JSON-schema `type` values and descriptions carrying `null to clear` semantics;
  - the cron tool schema has an explicit guard against `anyOf`, type arrays, `not`, and `const` leaking back into provider-facing tool schema;
  - prompt snapshots were regenerated from the final schema;
  - the durable-final compatibility alias has an explicit `@deprecated` tag.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/release-beta-verifier.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts` -> 2 files, 42 tests passed.
- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` -> cron tool 84 tests passed; cron schema 15 tests passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` -> prompt snapshots current, 7 files.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test --pretty false` -> passed.
- `pnpm check:architecture` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- Full prior-failing shard reproduction: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-agents-tools OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts` -> 53 files, 860 tests passed.
- Core-fast local reproduction: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=core-unit-fast OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast-fake-timers.config.ts` -> 2 shards passed; unit-fast shard 1053 files / 9993 tests passed, fake-timer shard 10 files / 140 tests passed.
- Earlier exact CI shard reproduction for prior remote flake: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp> OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-control-plane-startup-health-runtime OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` -> 7 files, 67 tests passed.
- `ruby -e "require 'yaml'; YAML.load_file('.github/workflows/openclaw-release-publish.yml'); puts 'source yaml ok'"` -> `source yaml ok`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Beta release publish now fails closed before npm/plugin dispatch when the signed desktop beta distribution is not live, so beta npm cannot get ahead of the macOS Sparkle update channel.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw source checkout at commit `050fa4d197b87875d7ac38f7cee17e782fa6e458`, Node/pnpm repo environment, authenticated `gh` against the real `openclaw/openclaw` GitHub release state.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm release:verify-beta -- 2026.5.31-beta.4 --tag v2026.5.31-beta.4 --repo openclaw/openclaw --desktop-only` from the patched checkout.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the real command showed the new gate inspecting live GitHub release assets and live `appcast-beta.xml`:

```text
$ pnpm release:verify-beta -- 2026.5.31-beta.4 --tag v2026.5.31-beta.4 --repo openclaw/openclaw --desktop-only
Desktop beta distribution is incomplete:
  - GitHub release is missing desktop asset OpenClaw-2026.5.31-beta.4.zip.
  - GitHub release is missing desktop asset OpenClaw-2026.5.31-beta.4.dmg.
  - GitHub release is missing desktop asset OpenClaw-2026.5.31-beta.4.dSYM.zip.
  - desktop beta appcast unavailable: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast-beta.xml returned HTTP 404.
```

- Observed result after fix: The desktop-only verifier refused to pass because the signed beta desktop assets and `appcast-beta.xml` are absent; this is the intended fail-closed behavior that `OpenClaw Release Publish` now runs before any beta npm/plugin dispatch.
- What was not tested: A successful end-to-end signed macOS promote was not run from this account because `openclaw/releases` still reports READ permission here and the `mac-release` signing/notary/Sparkle secrets are maintainer-custodied.

## Release-infra dependency
The matching release-infra branch is `reidmcgill/releases:codex/signed-beta-appcast`. This account still has READ on `openclaw/releases`, so upstream PR creation/merge and workflow dispatch require an upstream maintainer.

target.title: Add Swedish control UI locale
target.body:
## Summary
- add Swedish (`sv`) as a supported control UI locale
- generate and register `ui/src/i18n/locales/sv.ts` against the current 1118-key source locale set
- preserve the newer `ar`, `it`, `vi`, `nl`, and `fa` locale registrations while syncing metadata and language labels
- update registry tests for the expanded locale list and Swedish browser locale detection, including `sv-SE`

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Swedish browser users with `sv` or `sv-SE` now resolve to the Swedish Control UI locale, and the lazy locale registry can load the real Swedish translation module.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `feat/control-ui-swedish-locale` at `cd3be8c14b`, rebased onto upstream `main` (`9e4eca00ff`), running Node v22.22.2 with repo dependencies installed by pnpm.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Imported the actual Control UI registry and Swedish locale module from the checked-out branch and resolved Swedish browser language values without mocks:

```console
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { resolveNavigatorLocale, loadLazyLocaleTranslation, SUPPORTED_LOCALES } from "./ui/src/i18n/lib/registry.ts"; const sv=await loadLazyLocaleTranslation("sv"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ hasSv: SUPPORTED_LOCALES.includes("sv"), svSE: resolveNavigatorLocale("sv-SE"), sv: resolveNavigatorLocale("sv"), label: sv?.languages?.sv, commonHealth: sv?.common?.health }, null, 2));'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real local checkout:

```console
{
  "hasSv": true,
  "svSE": "sv",
  "sv": "sv",
  "label": "Svenska",
  "commonHealth": "Status"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The runtime registry includes `sv`, `resolveNavigatorLocale("sv-SE")` returns `sv`, `resolveNavigatorLocale("sv")` returns `sv`, and the loaded Swedish locale exposes Swedish UI strings.

**What was not tested:** No known gaps for this registry/lazy-locale behavior. Full browser screenshot proof was not added because this change is in the locale registry and generated locale data rather than a visual layout path.

## Validation
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- committed diff credential-pattern scan found no secret material

## Notes
- the Swedish locale now matches the current source key shape; untranslated newer strings are tracked as fallback keys by the existing i18n metadata flow

target.title: fix(discord): reconnect after abnormal gateway close
target.body:
## Summary
- re-arm Discord gateway reconnect after abnormal non-intentional closes when shouldReconnect had been cleared
- preserve intentional disconnect and normal close behavior
- add regression coverage for abnormal 1006 recovery and intentional disconnect suppression

## Retarget note (2026-06-10)
- Base changed `release/2026.5.20` → `main`: the original base was a stale release branch that predates `scripts/github/dependency-guard.mjs`, which made the dependency-guard checks structurally unable to run. Landing on `main` also makes the fix durable across future releases.
- Branch rebuilt as `main` + a single cherry-picked fix commit (no release-prep history). Diff is 2 files, +61/−1: `extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts` — 27/27 pass on the current head (includes the three regression tests).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.lifecycle.test.ts` — 21/21 pass.

## Ops note
- Do not hot-patch or restart the live /usr/local OpenClaw gateway for this PR.
- Live runtime adoption should happen through the normal reviewed release/upgrade path after merge.
- Paperclip: SUP-2240


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Discord gateway lane stays silently dead after an abnormal websocket close (code 1006) when `shouldReconnect` was stale-false: the close handler returned without re-arming reconnect, so the lane never recovered until a manual gateway restart, while `/health` kept reporting healthy.

**Real environment tested:** Production OpenClaw gateway on macOS (launchd service), live Discord bot lane (@Jarvis, application id 1470466195231604919) on a real Discord server. The identical close-handler re-arm logic in this PR was applied to the installed dist runtime; the comparison below is patched vs unpatched on the same machine, same bot, same network.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Applied the same close-handler re-arm logic to the installed runtime at `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/`, validated syntax with `node --check`, restarted the gateway with `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$UID/ai.openclaw.gateway`, confirmed `curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/health` returned live, then tailed `~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.log` across nine days of real Discord traffic to observe behavior on every abnormal `1006` close.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted runtime log excerpts from `~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.log`. Patched window 2026-06-01 → 2026-06-09: 35 abnormal `code=1006` closes, every one followed by silent automatic recovery — zero dead-lane incidents, zero manual restarts. Sample (each later close proves a new websocket had been established in between):

```
2026-06-08T10:09:28.805-04:00 [discord] gateway websocket closed flow=4910b473-... code=1006 reasonBytes=0 reason=<empty>
2026-06-08T10:09:28.806-04:00 [discord] gateway: Gateway websocket closed: 1006
2026-06-08T10:50:33.923-04:00 [discord] gateway websocket closed flow=0e02da49-... code=1006 reasonBytes=0 reason=<empty>
2026-06-08T11:47:25.168-04:00 [discord] gateway websocket closed flow=5b2298e2-... code=1006 reasonBytes=0 reason=<empty>
```

Contrast — unpatched regression window (a 2026-06-09 upgrade replaced the patched dist with stock code): within ~36h the original bug reproduced — `1006` with stale `shouldReconnect=false`, no re-arm, Discord lane silently dead for 88 minutes while `/health` kept returning `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`:

```
2026-06-11T05:43:42.319-04:00 [discord] gateway websocket closed flow=eab0cd6f-... code=1006 reasonBytes=0 reason=<empty>
2026-06-11T05:43:42.320-04:00 [discord] gateway: Gateway websocket closed: 1006
   ... no [discord] activity for 88 minutes (lane dead, /health still ok) ...
2026-06-11T07:11:24.293-04:00 [gateway] http server listening (12 plugins: ... discord ...)   <- manual launchctl kickstart
2026-06-11T07:11:25.914-04:00 [discord] [default] Discord bot probe resolved @Jarvis
2026-06-11T07:11:27.661-04:00 [discord] client initialized as 1470466195231604919; awaiting gateway readiness
```

**Observed result after fix:** Every abnormal 1006 close during the nine-day patched window was followed by automatic reconnect within seconds; Discord messages kept flowing and no manual `launchctl kickstart` was needed. With the stock (unpatched) runtime, the very same close condition left the lane dead until a manual restart — confirming the change alters real behavior, not just internals. The patched logic was reapplied to the live runtime on 2026-06-11 07:11 EDT and the lane recovered immediately; this recurring wipe-on-upgrade is why the fix needs to land upstream.

**What was not tested:** Live behavior for fatal Discord close codes (4004, 4010–4014) and sharded/multi-shard gateway setups — those code paths are unchanged by this PR. Intentional `disconnect()` suppression was exercised in real use only via normal gateway shutdowns and restarts.

target.title: fix(memory-core): clear daily-ingestion sqlite namespace on dreaming repair
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`repairDreamingArtifacts()` (the `openclaw memory status --fix` repair path) is supposed to reset dreaming ingestion to a clean, rebuildable state, but it only clears the `dreaming-session-ingestion-files` and `dreaming-session-ingestion-seen` SQLite namespaces — it never clears the migrated `dreaming-daily-ingestion` namespace. This is the repair-side counterpart of the audit change in #92020 (fixes #92017), which taught `auditDreamingArtifacts()` to treat **all three** ingestion namespaces (including `dreaming-daily-ingestion`) as "ingestion state present". Because the repair was not updated to match, the `--fix` flow immediately re-audits and still reports `sessionIngestionExists: true` solely because the daily-ingestion rows survive — a self-contradicting phantom state right after a "successful" repair — and the stale daily-ingestion bookkeeping leaks past the repair, so daily memory files are not re-ingested on the next sweep. Affected: anyone running `memory status --fix` after the daily/session ingestion sidecars were migrated to SQLite.

## Evidence

Proof drives the **real exported `auditDreamingArtifacts` / `repairDreamingArtifacts`** against the **real SQLite-backed plugin-state store** (`node:sqlite`, real `openclaw.sqlite` on disk via a temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`); the read-back crosses the disk boundary via an **independent read-only `node:sqlite` handle** counting `dreaming-daily-ingestion` rows in `plugin_state_entries`. No production logic mocked.

- **Before fix (patched tree, on-disk round-trip)**: daily rows on disk `1 → 0` after repair; the immediate `--fix` re-audit flips `sessionIngestionExists` from `true` → `false`.
- **Negative control** (`dreaming-repair.ts` reverted to `HEAD~1`, daily-clear removed, identical script): daily rows survive on disk `1 → 1` and the re-audit still reports the phantom `sessionIngestionExists: true` — isolating the daily-clear line as exactly what closes the gap.
- **Regression tests** (`extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.test.ts`): `clears sqlite daily ingestion state when archiving session corpus` and `re-audit reports ingestion absent after repairing daily-only ingestion state`. Vitest on the patched tree: **9 passed (7 existing + 2 new)**; reverting the fix fails exactly those 2 new tests (`expected true to be false` on the re-audit).

Full proof transcript (commands, on-disk SQLite output, negative-control output) is preserved verbatim under **Real behavior proof** below.

## Summary

- **Problem**: `repairDreamingArtifacts()` (driven by `openclaw memory status --fix`) archives the dreaming session corpus and clears the migrated SQLite ingestion state, but it only clears the `dreaming-session-ingestion-files` and `dreaming-session-ingestion-seen` namespaces. It never clears the migrated `dreaming-daily-ingestion` namespace.
- **Why it matters**: This is the repair-side counterpart of the audit change merged in #92020 (fixes #92017). That PR taught `auditDreamingArtifacts()` to treat **all three** ingestion namespaces — including `dreaming-daily-ingestion` — as "ingestion state present" after the legacy JSON sidecars were migrated to SQLite. The repair path was not updated to match, so:
  1. After a repair, the `memory status --fix` flow immediately re-audits and still reports `sessionIngestionExists: true` solely because the daily-ingestion rows survive — a self-contradicting phantom state right after a "successful" repair.
  2. The daily-ingestion bookkeeping (which daily memory files were already ingested) leaks past the repair, so the daily files are not re-ingested on the next sweep. This breaks the repair's intent of resetting dreaming ingestion to a clean rebuildable state.
- The legacy sidecars are siblings by design: `dreaming-phases.ts` lists `daily-ingestion.json` and `session-ingestion.json` side by side as the legacy ingestion JSON files, and issue #92017 archives both together.

## Changes

- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.ts` — `clearSessionIngestionState()` now also clears `DREAMING_DAILY_INGESTION_NAMESPACE`, alongside the existing session files/seen namespaces. (The constant was already imported by #92020.) Added a comment documenting that all three ingestion namespaces must be cleared together to stay symmetric with the audit.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.test.ts` — two regression tests:
  - `clears sqlite daily ingestion state when archiving session corpus` — asserts the daily namespace is emptied after repair.
  - `re-audit reports ingestion absent after repairing daily-only ingestion state` — reproduces the CLI `--fix` audit → repair → re-audit flow and asserts the re-audit no longer reports a phantom ingestion state.

No change to the dreaming pipeline, the migration, or the CLI output formatting.

## Real behavior proof

**Label**: proof: sufficient

**Behavior addressed**: `repairDreamingArtifacts()` (the `openclaw memory status --fix` repair path) must clear the migrated `dreaming-daily-ingestion` SQLite namespace when it archives the dreaming session corpus, so the rows actually disappear from the SQLite state DB on disk and the immediate re-audit performed by `--fix` no longer reports a phantom `sessionIngestionExists: true`. Before this patch the daily-ingestion rows survive the repair and the re-audit contradicts itself; after it, the rows are gone and the re-audit reports ingestion absent.

**Real environment tested**: local, Linux, Node v22.22.0, `node --import tsx`. The proof imports the **real exported `auditDreamingArtifacts` / `repairDreamingArtifacts`** directly from the changed `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.ts` and configures the **real SQLite-backed plugin-state store** (`node:sqlite` is the real backend) against a real on-disk state DB by pointing `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` at a fresh temp dir. No production logic is mocked. The read-back assertion crosses the real disk boundary: after the repair runs, an **independent read-only `node:sqlite` handle is opened directly on the `openclaw.sqlite` file** and queries `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id = 'memory-core' AND namespace = 'dreaming-daily-ingestion'` — so the assertion reads what is physically persisted in the SQLite file, not an in-process return value.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. `git worktree add` PR head `fix/dreaming-repair-clear-daily-ingestion`.
2. `TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=tsconfig.json node --import tsx proof.mts`, where `proof.mts`: (a) sets `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` to a temp dir, (b) writes one `dreaming-daily-ingestion` entry for a temp workspace via the real store + creates a `memory/.dreams/session-corpus` file, (c) counts the daily rows on disk through an independent read-only `node:sqlite` handle, (d) runs the real `auditDreamingArtifacts`, (e) runs the real `repairDreamingArtifacts`, (f) re-counts the daily rows on disk through a fresh independent handle, (g) runs the real `auditDreamingArtifacts` again (the `--fix` re-audit).
3. Negative control: `git checkout HEAD~1 -- extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.ts` (reverts only the daily-clear), re-run the identical script, then restore with `git checkout HEAD -- …`.
4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.test.ts`.

**Evidence after fix** (patched tree, real on-disk SQLite round-trip):
```text
  state db path = /tmp/proof-93389-CRAUjg/state/openclaw.sqlite
== STEP 1: daily rows on disk BEFORE repair (independent sqlite handle) ==
  plugin_state_entries[memory-core/dreaming-daily-ingestion] = 1
== STEP 2: real auditDreamingArtifacts BEFORE repair ==
  sessionIngestionExists = true
== STEP 3: real repairDreamingArtifacts (archives corpus + clears SQLite) ==
  archivedSessionCorpus = true
  warnings = []
== STEP 4: daily rows on disk AFTER repair (independent sqlite handle) ==
  plugin_state_entries[memory-core/dreaming-daily-ingestion] = 0
== STEP 5: real auditDreamingArtifacts AFTER repair (CLI --fix re-audit) ==
  sessionIngestionExists = false
== RESULT ==
PASS: daily-ingestion namespace cleared from disk; re-audit no longer reports phantom ingestion state
```

Negative control (`dreaming-repair.ts` reverted to `HEAD~1` — daily clear removed), identical script:
```text
== STEP 4: daily rows on disk AFTER repair (independent sqlite handle) ==
  plugin_state_entries[memory-core/dreaming-daily-ingestion] = 1
== STEP 5: real auditDreamingArtifacts AFTER repair (CLI --fix re-audit) ==
  sessionIngestionExists = true
== RESULT ==
FAIL: daily-ingestion namespace not fully cleared / audit still reports ingestion present
```

Vitest (patched tree): `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 9 passed (9)` (7 existing + 2 new).

**Observed result after fix**: with the patch, the daily-ingestion row is physically deleted from the `openclaw.sqlite` file by the repair (disk count `1 → 0`), and the immediate `--fix` re-audit flips from `sessionIngestionExists: true` to `false`. With the daily-clear reverted (`HEAD~1`), the exact same script shows the row **survives on disk** (`1 → 1`) and the re-audit still reports the phantom `true` — confirming the daily-clear line is precisely what closes the symmetric gap and that the audit/repair are otherwise unchanged. The two new regression tests pin the same behavior in vitest; reverting the fix fails exactly those two.

**What was not tested**: this proof does not run the full `openclaw memory status --fix` CLI command end-to-end (it drives the two real exported functions the CLI calls, plus the real SQLite store and an independent on-disk read-back); it also does not exercise the subsequent daily-file *re-ingestion* sweep that consumes the now-cleared namespace (out of scope — the fix only resets the bookkeeping). Failure handling of `clearMemoryCoreWorkspaceNamespace` (the existing try/catch warning path) was not fault-injected.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.test.ts` → 9 passed (7 existing + 2 new).
- Negative control: with the daily-clear reverted, exactly the 2 new tests fail (`expected true to be false` on the re-audit), the other 7 pass — the tests pin the bug.

## Risk checklist

- **Blast radius**: One private helper in `dreaming-repair.ts`; only adds a clear of an already-imported namespace. No public API/signature change.
- **Behavioral risk**: Repair already clears the two sibling ingestion namespaces and archives the daily-ingestion JSON sidecar on the filesystem side; clearing the SQLite daily namespace makes the SQLite path consistent with both. Clearing is idempotent and wrapped in the existing try/catch that records a warning on failure.
- **Backward compatibility**: Repair only runs under explicit `--fix`; an empty daily-ingestion namespace simply causes daily memory files to be re-ingested on the next sweep, which is the intended post-repair behavior.

<!-- codex-rescue-machine-readable-real-behavior-proof -->
## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repairDreamingArtifacts, the openclaw memory status --fix repair path, must clear the migrated dreaming-daily-ingestion SQLite namespace when it archives the dreaming session corpus. Before this patch the daily-ingestion rows survived repair and the immediate re-audit still reported phantom sessionIngestionExists=true.

Real environment tested: Local Linux runtime with Node v22.22.0 and node --import tsx, importing the real exported auditDreamingArtifacts and repairDreamingArtifacts from extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.ts. The proof uses the real SQLite-backed plugin-state store with OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR pointing at a fresh temp directory and verifies persisted rows through an independent read-only node:sqlite handle.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: In PR head fix/dreaming-repair-clear-daily-ingestion, ran TSX_TSCONFIG_PATH=tsconfig.json node --import tsx proof.mts, then ran a negative control by reverting only extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.ts to HEAD~1 and rerunning the same proof, then restored the file. Also ran node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-repair.test.ts.

Evidence after fix: Live terminal output from the real on-disk SQLite round trip:
```text
state db path = /tmp/proof-93389-CRAUjg/state/openclaw.sqlite
== STEP 1: daily rows on disk BEFORE repair (independent sqlite handle) ==
  plugin_state_entries[memory-core/dreaming-daily-ingestion] = 1
== STEP 2: real auditDreamingArtifacts BEFORE repair ==
  sessionIngestionExists = true
== STEP 3: real repairDreamingArtifacts (archives corpus + clears SQLite) ==
  archivedSessionCorpus = true
  warnings = []
== STEP 4: daily rows on disk AFTER repair (independent sqlite handle) ==
  plugin_state_entries[memory-core/dreaming-daily-ingestion] = 0
== STEP 5: real auditDreamingArtifacts AFTER repair (CLI --fix re-audit) ==
  sessionIngestionExists = false
RESULT: PASS: daily-ingestion namespace cleared from disk; re-audit no longer reports phantom ingestion state
```
Negative control with the daily-clear removed showed the daily rows stayed 1 after repair and re-audit still returned sessionIngestionExists=true.

Observed result after fix: The daily-ingestion row is physically deleted from openclaw.sqlite by repair, changing the independent disk count from 1 to 0, and the immediate re-audit changes from sessionIngestionExists=true to false. Vitest passed 9/9 tests, including the two new regression tests; reverting the fix fails exactly those two.

What was not tested: The full openclaw memory status --fix CLI command was not run end to end; the proof drives the two real exported functions used by that path plus the real SQLite store and an independent disk read-back. The subsequent daily-file re-ingestion sweep was not exercised.

target.title: feat(plugins): add guard delivery and subagent review hooks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the current hook surface can gate tool execution, but it cannot synchronously guard tool results before the agent sees them and it has no explicit pre-spawn / pre-return subagent review phases.
- Why it matters: phase 1-3 guard work needs a typed decision model plus real observation and subagent boundaries, otherwise "auto mode" and third-party policy plugins sit on incomplete seams.
- What changed: added shared guard decision/annotation types, new `before_tool_result_deliver` / `before_subagent_spawn` / `before_subagent_result_deliver` hooks, wired them into tool-result persistence and subagent spawn/announce flows, and extended targeted tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add a guard kernel, model-backed policy runtime, or any bundled automode/firewall plugin yet.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): N/A
- Why this regressed now: N/A
- If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugins/wired-hooks-subagent.test.ts`, `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts`, `src/agents/sessions-spawn-hooks.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: guard decisions merge consistently, tool results can be warned/blocked before persistence, subagent spawn can be denied before launch, and child results can be rewritten before announce delivery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests hit the exact hook seams without needing the full policy stack.

target.title: feat(mxc): add Windows MXC sandbox backend
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw does not currently have an official installable MXC sandbox backend for Windows hosts that can run sandboxed commands through Microsoft eXecution Containers. This leaves MXC-capable Windows environments without a first-party plugin path for ProcessContainer-backed execution and filesystem bridge operations.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an official OpenShell-style external `mxc` sandbox plugin package (`@openclaw/mxc-sandbox`) with Windows-only readiness checks, plugin-owned MXC SDK dependency ownership, ProcessContainer payload construction, a plugin-side launcher, host-backed filesystem bridge support, sandbox policy loading, and targeted tests.

The plugin source lives under `extensions/mxc`, but it is packaged as an installable plugin (`bundledDist: false`) so the MXC SDK/native dependency is owned by the plugin package instead of an unshipped bundled `node_modules` tree. The backend uses the stable current MXC `0.7.0-alpha` schema and keeps sandbox defaults conservative: curated environments, headless UI, per-exec temp directories, absolute binary overrides, host-derived Windows roots, read-only default workspace access for `none`/`ro`, and fail-closed unsupported policy handling.

## User Impact

Users on MXC-capable Windows hosts can install the official MXC sandbox plugin, restart the Gateway, configure `sandbox.backend: "mxc"`, and run OpenClaw sandboxed commands through MXC. Non-Windows or unsupported Windows hosts remain unaffected because the plugin stays dormant when readiness probes fail.

Install command:

```bash
openclaw plugins install @openclaw/mxc-sandbox
```

## Evidence

- `pnpm test:extension mxc -- --reporter=verbose` — passed.
- `pnpm test extensions\mxc test\scripts\generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — passed.
- `pnpm test:changed` — passed.
- `pnpm release:plugins:npm:check -- --plugins @openclaw/mxc-sandbox` — passed; publishable plugin metadata looks OK.
- `pnpm release:plugins:clawhub:check -- --plugins @openclaw/mxc-sandbox` — passed; publishable plugin metadata looks OK.
- `pnpm check` — passed, including import cycle check with 0 runtime value cycles.
- `pnpm build` — passed.
- `node scripts\lib\plugin-npm-runtime-build.mjs extensions\mxc` plus package-manifest `npm pack --dry-run` — passed; package includes `dist/index.js`, `dist/mxc-spawn-launcher.mjs`, and MXC SDK binaries including `node_modules/@microsoft/mxc-sdk/bin/x64/wxc-exec.exe`.
- `pnpm deps:changes:report -- --base-ref origin/main --markdown <session>/dependency-changes.md --json <session>/dependency-changes.json` — generated dependency report.

Real Windows MXC package proof:

- Crabbox provider: `ssh`
- Lease/id: `static_10-127-3-94`
- Target: `windows-normal`
- Remote Node/npm: `node v26.4.0`, `npm 11.17.0`
- Built and packed `@openclaw/mxc-sandbox@2026.6.10` on the Windows host.
- Installed published `openclaw@2026.6.10` plus the local packed plugin in a fresh temp project.
- Verified package-owned runtime dependency path:
  - `PROOF:installedOpenClaw=2026.6.10`
  - `PROOF:installedPlugin=2026.6.10`
  - `PROOF:mxcSdk=0.7.0`
  - `PROOF:wxcExists=True ...\node_modules\@openclaw\mxc-sandbox\node_modules\@microsoft\mxc-sdk\bin\x64\wxc-exec.exe`
- Registered and ran backend `mxc`:
  - `PROOF:registeredServices=mxc-sandbox-cleanup`
  - `PROOF:backendId=mxc`
  - sandbox command: `echo MXC_PROOF_OK && cd`
  - `PROOF:exit=0`
  - `PROOF:stdout=MXC_PROOF_OK |...\workspace|`
  - `PROOF:stderr=`

User-facing plugin install proof on the same Windows host:

- Installed published `openclaw@2026.6.10` in an isolated temp project.
- Ran `node <published-openclaw>/dist/entry.js plugins install <local @openclaw/mxc-sandbox tgz> --force --pin`.
- Install succeeded:
  - `PROOF3:installExit=0`
  - install output included `Installing plugin dependencies…`, `Installed plugin: mxc`, and `Restart the gateway to load plugins.`
  - `PROOF3:configExists=True`
  - `PROOF3:configHasMxc=True`
  - `PROOF3:pluginRoot=...\state\extensions\mxc`
  - `PROOF3:installedPlugin=2026.6.10`

Workspace access proof after review 4588711229:

- Static SSH Windows host: `provider=ssh`, lease `static_10-127-3-94`, target `windows-normal`.
- `workspaceAccess: "none"` now keeps the MXC workdir read-only rather than read-write by default:
  - `PROOF4:backendId=mxc`
  - `PROOF4:workspaceAccess=none`
  - `PROOF4:workdirReadonly=true`
  - `PROOF4:workdirReadwrite=false`
- Command execution still succeeds with the read-only default workspace policy:
  - sandbox command: `echo MXC_NONE_PROOF_OK && cd`
  - `PROOF4:exit=0`
  - `PROOF4:stdout=MXC_NONE_PROOF_OK |...\workspace|`
  - `PROOF4:stderr=`

Full `pnpm test` was attempted earlier per CONTRIBUTING, but the broad local suite hit unrelated existing non-MXC failures in UI/TUI/agent runtime tests before completion. The changed/MXC-specific lanes above passed cleanly.

Copilot autoreview was attempted on the latest review-fix head. It produced a finding about `@microsoft/mxc-sdk@0.7.0` re-serializing config into `--config-base64` for `wxc-exec`; local source inspection confirmed that this is the current upstream SDK transport behavior and that the SDK exposes no non-argv transport for `spawnSandboxFromConfig`. No local OpenClaw-only patch is available without an upstream SDK/executor transport change.

## Notes

- Contributor changelog edit was removed per CONTRIBUTING.
- `.github/labeler.yml` maps `extensions/mxc/**` to `plugin: mxc`.
- Upstream label verification found no existing `plugin: mxc` label; label creation requires maintainer permissions.
- Dependency Graph Guard still requires maintainer/security `/allow-dependencies-change` approval for the current head SHA 7dc546ccb7ffc27830bbe1c932f58640662c52c7.

target.title: fix(ui): sync message delete to session transcript backend
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Clicking the delete button on a message group in the Control UI only hid the message visually (localStorage) but left the backend .jsonl transcript untouched. The AI could still see the "deleted" content on the next turn.
- **Why it matters:** Users believe a message has been permanently removed, but the AI continues to respond based on that content -- a data inconsistency that creates both a confusing UX and a potential privacy risk.
- **What changed:** Added `sessions.truncate` RPC method, wired it through the protocol layer, gateway handler, and UI so that deleting a message actually truncates the backend transcript from that seq onward.
- **What did NOT change:** Existing `sessions.compact`, `sessions.reset`, or any other session mutation paths are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History

- **Root cause:** The `onDelete` handler in the chat view only added the message group key to a localStorage-backed `DeletedMessages` set -- it never called any backend RPC to modify the .jsonl transcript.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No integration test verified that deleting a message in the UI actually affected the transcript file.
- **Prior context:** The `sessions.compact` and `sessions.reset` RPC methods already existed with full protocol/handler/UI wiring; `truncate` was simply never added.
- **Why this regressed now:** The feature was never implemented; localStorage-only deletion was the original (incomplete) approach.

## Changes

### Backend (`src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`)

Three-phase truncate with full safety:

1. **Phase 1** -- Read-only scan under `acquireSessionWriteLock` to locate the cut point. ENOENT-guarded. Does NOT abort active runs yet.
2. **Phase 2** -- Only if a valid cut exists: `cleanupSessionBeforeMutation` aborts active runs with `reason: "session-delete"`. If cleanup fails, the handler returns an error and the transcript is left untouched.
3. **Phase 3c** -- Re-acquire write lock, re-scan for seq (guards against concurrent `sessions.compact`), write-then-rename: writes truncated content to `.tmp.*`, renames original to `.bak.*`, renames temp to original. Preserves original file mode (`statSync(filePath).mode & 0o777`).

Additional safety:
- `rejectWebchatSessionMutation` gate blocks webchat clients (matching `sessions.patch`/`sessions.delete`)
- `reason: "truncate"` in `emitSessionsChanged` (not "compact")
- Token counts cleared on the session store entry after truncation

### Protocol layer

- `SessionsTruncateParamsSchema` with `key` (NonEmptyString) and `seq` (Integer, minimum: 1)
- AJV validator (`validateSessionsTruncateParams`) and type export
- Registered in `ProtocolSchemas`, `server-methods-list.ts`, and `method-scopes.ts` (operator.write)

### UI (`ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` + `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`)

- `onTruncateHistory(seq, onFail)` returns `boolean`: `false` when disconnected (caller skips optimistic hide), `true` when RPC dispatched
- `onFail` callback: called on RPC error OR `truncated: false`; caller's `deleted.restore(item.key)` reverses the optimistic hide
- Session-scoped: captures `state.sessionKey` at dispatch time; ignores `.then`/`.catch` if user navigated away
- Run-ID aware: captures `state.chatRunId` at dispatch; if a new run started during RPC flight, still reloads but does not clear the new run's state
- Only clears `chatMessages`/`chatStream`/`chatRunId` when `result.truncated === true`
- `.catch` does NOT clear chat history (only sets `lastError`)
- Search mode: delete disabled entirely when search is active (merged groups could silently drop non-adjacent history)
- When `__openclaw.seq` metadata is absent (e.g. live-streaming messages before history reload), `onDelete` is a no-op -- the user can retry after the chat reloads

### Swift models

- `SessionsTruncateParams` added to both `apps/macos` and `apps/shared` GatewayModels.swift

## Review Issues Addressed

All P1 and P2 issues from Greptile, Copilot, and Codex reviews have been addressed:

| # | Severity | Issue | Resolution |
|---|----------|-------|------------|
| 1 | P1 | Transcript loss if writeFileSync fails after rename | Write-then-rename: .tmp.* then .bak.* then rename |
| 2 | P1 | Abort active runs before truncating | Phase 2 cleanupSessionBeforeMutation after Phase 1 confirms cut |
| 3 | P1 | Block webchat clients from truncating | rejectWebchatSessionMutation gate |
| 4 | P1 | Preserve transcript file mode | statSync mode passed to writeFileSync |
| 5 | P1 | Avoid local-only delete fallback when seq missing | onDelete is no-op when seq absent |
| 6 | P1 | Compute seq from clicked msg not group head | Delete disabled in search mode |
| 7 | P1 | Serialize truncation with concurrent appends | acquireSessionWriteLock in Phase 1 and Phase 3c |
| 8 | P1 | Avoid returning failure after truncation committed | Cleanup before file write |
| 9 | P1 | Propagate cleanup failures | Cleanup error returns before file modification |
| 10 | P2 | Silent failure when disconnected | Returns false; caller skips optimistic hide |
| 11 | P2 | reason: "compact" for truncate event | Changed to "truncate" |
| 12 | P2 | Defer local delete until truncate confirmed | Boolean return controls optimistic hide |
| 13 | P2 | Avoid aborting on no-op | Phase 1 confirms cut before Phase 2 abort |
| 14 | P2 | Scope callback to initiating session | Session key captured at dispatch |
| 15 | P2 | Don't clear newer run state | Run-ID guard |
| 16 | P2 | Recompute cutpoint under final lock | Phase 3c re-scans under lock |
| 17 | P2 | Don't gate refresh on unchanged run ID | loadChatHistory called regardless |
| 18 | P2 | Preserve run state on no-op | Only clears when truncated: true |
| 19 | P2 | Restore optimistic hide on no-op/error | onFail() before session guard |
| 20 | P2 | Re-check before aborting | Phase 1 before Phase 2 |
| 21 | P2 | Reload even if newer run starts | loadChatHistory in new-run branch |
| 22 | P2 | Handle ENOENT in phase-1 scan | ENOENT catch with fallback |
| 23 | P2 | Restore delete when off-session | onFail() always called first |
| 24 | P2 | Preserve chat history on RPC fail | .catch only sets lastError |
| 25 | P2 | Comment refers to fromSeq | Updated to p.seq |

## Regression Test Plan

- All 30 method-scope classification tests pass
- All 37 protocol tests pass
- All 38 gateway sessions integration tests pass
- `pnpm build` passes cleanly
- `pnpm format` clean

target.title: fix/agents-primary-model-selection-and-catalog-loading-41344
target.body: ## Summary - Fixes incorrect Primary Model selection when switching between agents in Agents > Overview. - Fixes partial / blank Primary Model dropdowns after refresh or first load by ensuring the model catalog is available on Agents Overview paths. - Adds targeted tests for both selection stability and catalog hydration behavior. - Keeps the fix scoped to the Agents Overview model-selection path. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #41344 - Related #46275 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: 1. The agent details subtree could reuse stale DOM/select state when switching agents, which caused the Primary Model select to drift toward the previously viewed agent's state. 2. Agents Overview depended on a shared model catalog that was not guaranteed to be loaded on refresh / first-load / overview-only paths, so the select could render before the catalog was ready and degrade into a partial or blank-looking list. - Missing detection / guardrail: - Existing coverage covered some selected-value behavior, but it did not cover model-catalog hydration timing or the refresh/overview path where the catalog starts empty and arrives later. - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Prior work around #41344 / #46275 addressed the agent-switching selection issue. Revalidating that fix on latest `upstream/main` exposed a second, related lifecycle issue in Agents Overview model-catalog loading. - Why this regressed now: - The Overview UI depends on model-catalog data, but not all entry paths guaranteed that the catalog was loaded before rendering. - If unknown, what was ruled out: - Ruled out a pure selected-state-only explanation. The partial/blank dropdown after refresh was caused by catalog availability/lifecycle, not just DOM selection drift. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.browser.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: - Switching between agents keeps the correct explicit Primary Model selected. - The inherit-default option text does not go blank. - When the model catalog is initially empty, Agents Overview shows a loading state instead of a misleading partial list. - When the catalog hydrates later, the full list appears without losing the selected value. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: - The bug spans both UI render behavior and shared model-catalog lifecycle, so a combination of focused controller/unit tests plus browser-level select behavior tests is the smallest reliable coverage. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): - Existing browser coverage covered some selection behavior, but not the catalog hydration path. - If no new test is added, why not: - N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). If none, write `None`. - Switching between agents no longer causes the Primary Model select to show the wrong agent's value. - On refresh / first entry to Agents Overview, the Primary Model select no longer degrades into a misleading partial list. - While the model catalog is still loading, the UI now shows a loading state instead of pretending only a small subset of options exists. - Once the catalog loads, the full option list appears without losing the current selection. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: - N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Windows 10 - Runtime/container: local dev environment - Model/provider: local UI validation only - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): standard Agents UI configuration with default and non-default agents ### Steps 1. Open Agents > Overview > Model Selection > Primary Model. 2. Switch between a default agent and a non-default agent. 3. Refresh the page and reopen the Primary Model select immediately. 4. Optionally wait for model-catalog hydration or switch away/back to Overview. ### Expected - The select reflects the correct explicit value for the current agent. - The inherit-default option text is stable. - Refresh / first-load does not show a misleading partial catalog. - Once the catalog is available, the full model list appears without losing the selected value. ### Actual - Before this fix, switching agents could retain stale select state from another agent. - Before this fix, refresh / first-load could show only fallback options (for example inherit/current) or appear blank until the catalog became available later. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ### UI Evidence #### Before After refresh / first load, the Primary Model dropdown could show only fallback entries or appear blank. #### After After the fix, the Primary Model dropdown restores the full option list correctly after refresh / first load, without losing the selected value. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Switched repeatedly between default and non-default agents and confirmed Primary Model stays tied to the current agent. - Refreshed the page and reopened Agents Overview to verify the select no longer falls into a misleading partial/blank state. - Confirmed catalog hydration restores the full list without losing the selected value. - Edge cases checked: - Inherit-default text path - Explicit non-default model selection - Refresh / first-load / return-to-overview behavior - What you did **not** verify: - Full end-to-end coverage across unrelated panels/modules - Additional stale/error UX beyond this fix's loading-state behavior ## AI Assistance - This PR was developed with AI assistance (CodeX). - I reviewed the resulting changes, reproduced the bug locally, and verified the final behavior manually. - Testing level: fully tested locally with targeted automated tests and manual UI verification. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: - N/A ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: - Revert this PR's two commits. - Files/config to restore: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-overview.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-settings.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - Primary Model shows another agent's value after switching agents - Refresh / first-load shows only partial model options or a blank-looking select - Catalog hydration changes the list but drops the selected value ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: - Multiple UI entry points now ensure model-catalog availability, which could increase repeated refresh attempts if not properly deduped. - Mitigation: - The loading/ensure logic is centralized in the shared model controller and covered by targeted tests. - Risk: - Keyed subtree remounting can discard unsaved local UI state when switching agents. - Mitigation: - That tradeoff is intentional here because correctness of agent-specific model selection is more important than preserving stale subtree state across agent switches.

target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.




## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `tools.fs.roots` lets an operator grant an agent narrowly-scoped host filesystem access — an allowlist of roots, each `dir`/`file` with `ro`/`rw` access. Host-mode read/write/edit (and apply_patch) must allow paths inside an allowed root, deny paths outside every root, and reject writes to `ro` roots.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b4056fc332` (Node 22, macOS), driving the production host-mode filesystem tools against a real on-disk temporary directory tree (real `fs` syscalls; no fixtures or stubs).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a real temp tree (an `rw` root `shared-rw`, an `ro` root `shared-ro`, and a `secret/` directory outside every root with a real file in it), built the production tool set via `createOpenClawCodingTools({ config: { tools: { fs: { roots: [...] } } } })`, and executed the resulting `read`/`write` tools against each path with `node --import tsx`. Live console output below.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
config tools.fs.roots:
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-rw, kind: dir, access: rw }
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro, kind: dir, access: ro }
workspace (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/workspace
forbidden dir (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret

=== host-mode reads ===
READ  inside rw root  (shared-rw/notes.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "rw-root content"
READ  inside ro root  (shared-ro/policy.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "ro-root content"
READ  OUTSIDE roots   (secret/passwords.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/passwords.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots

=== host-mode writes ===
WRITE inside rw root  (shared-rw/created.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, on disk: true
WRITE inside ro root  (shared-ro/hack.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro/hack.txt' is inside read-only root '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro'  (on disk: false)
WRITE OUTSIDE roots   (secret/exfil.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/exfil.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots  (on disk: false)

verify: secret/passwords.txt still says: "TOP SECRET — outside every allowed root"
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Reads inside the `rw` and `ro` roots returned the real file contents. A read of `secret/passwords.txt` (outside every root) was denied with `outside allowed filesystem roots`. A write inside the `rw` root landed on disk (`on disk: true`); a write to the `ro` root was denied (`inside read-only root`) and a write outside every root was denied — both left nothing on disk (`on disk: false`), and the out-of-root secret file was byte-for-byte unchanged afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Sandbox mode is out of scope by design — sandbox ignores `tools.fs.roots` and relies on Docker isolation — so it was not exercised here. No live LLM agent loop was run (no provider key in this environment); the proof drives the exact production tool layer the agent calls, against a real filesystem, rather than a full conversational turn.

target.title: fix(cron): stop fallback attempts when cron budget is exhausted
target.body:
## Problem

When a cron job's primary model times out near the cron deadline, the fallback model is started but immediately killed by the same cron timeout — receiving effectively 0ms to make an LLM request. The fallback never gets a fair chance to respond, and the user sees a misleading `cron: job execution timed out` error attributed to the fallback model.

**Observed in production logs (2026-03-22):**

```
[08:10:30.733] anthropic/claude-opus-4-6 attempt=1/2 reason=unknown err='Request was aborted.' duration=28667ms
[08:10:30.735] openai-codex/gpt-5.4    attempt=2/2 reason=timeout err='cron: job execution timed out' duration=0ms
```

The cron timeout wraps the entire fallback chain. When the primary model consumes most of the budget, the fallback model is allocated ~0ms and fails immediately.

## Solution

1. **Propagate the cron deadline** (`deadlineAtMs`) from `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` through the cron service state down to `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn`.

2. **Add a `beforeAttempt` hook** to `runWithModelFallback` that is called before each candidate attempt. If it returns `{ type: 'stop' }`, the fallback chain stops and preserves the original error from the previous attempt.

3. **Check remaining budget** in the cron layer's `beforeAttempt` callback. If less than `min(30s, timeout/4)` remains before the cron deadline, the fallback is skipped with a clear message:
   > `Skipping fallback: only 50ms remain before cron timeout (need at least 30000ms).`

This ensures:
- The primary model's real error is preserved (not masked by a fake cron timeout on the fallback)
- No wasted LLM API calls that would be aborted instantly
- The `beforeAttempt` hook is generic and reusable for other pre-attempt checks

## Changes

- `src/agents/model-fallback.ts` — Add `beforeAttempt` hook + `stop_before_candidate` decision type
- `src/cron/service/timer.ts` — Compute and pass `deadlineAtMs` to job execution
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` — Use `beforeAttempt` to check remaining cron budget
- `src/cron/service/state.ts` + `src/gateway/server-cron.ts` — Thread `deadlineAtMs` through interfaces

## Tests

- `model-fallback.test.ts` — beforeAttempt stop behavior
- `run.fallback-time-budget.test.ts` — Cron deadline propagation + fallback budget exhaustion
- `service.issue-regressions.test.ts` — Deadline passed to isolated runs


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron isolated agent-turn jobs should not start a model fallback when the primary attempt has consumed almost the whole cron deadline and the fallback would be killed immediately by the same timeout.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/cron-fallback-timeout` at `008791fa10`, macOS host, Node 22. The proof command uses the same fallback budget formula added in this branch and the cron job shape from the affected isolated agent-turn surface.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node .artifacts/pr-52365-real-behavior-proof.mjs` locally after the patch, then ran `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.fallback-time-budget.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` as supplemental coverage for the same gate.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the local reproduction command:

```
$ node .artifacts/pr-52365-real-behavior-proof.mjs
cron fallback budget repro
job: sessionTarget=isolated wakeMode=next-heartbeat payload=agentTurn timeoutSeconds=60
primary attempt: consumed 59900ms of a 60000ms cron deadline
fallback check: remainingMs=100; fallbackMinRemainingMs=15000
before(upstream/main): runWithModelFallback had no beforeAttempt budget gate -> fallbackAttempted=true
after(this branch): fallbackAttempted=false
after stop: Skipping fallback: only 100ms remain before cron timeout (need at least 15000ms).
```

Supplemental targeted test output:

```
$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.fallback-time-budget.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
✓ allows fallback attempts when the per-attempt budget fits inside the remaining cron deadline
✓ uses agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds for fallback budget when job timeoutSeconds is unset
✓ stops new fallback attempts when the per-attempt budget no longer fits
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
```

**Observed result after fix:** With only 100ms left before the cron deadline and a 15,000ms minimum fallback budget, the after-fix gate returns `fallbackAttempted=false` and reports `Skipping fallback: only 100ms remain before cron timeout (need at least 15000ms).` The before behavior had no `beforeAttempt` budget gate, so the fallback candidate was started even though it could only receive about 0ms.

**What was not tested:** Live Anthropic/OpenAI API calls were not run for this proof; the local reproduction covers the cron fallback budget decision and the targeted test covers the runtime hook path with mocked provider execution.

target.title: cron: reject webhook URLs with embedded credentials
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Cron webhook URLs could include embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.com/hook), which can leak secrets in logs, config, or URLs. - Why it matters: URL-embedded credentials are discouraged and can expose secrets; using query params or headers is preferred. - What changed: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl now rejects URLs with username or password in the parsed URL. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to auth headers, query params, or other credential handling; only http(s)://user:pass@host URLs are rejected. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Cron webhook URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:secret@host/path) are now rejected as invalid. URLs without credentials continue to work. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin) - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): CRON webhook delivery - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Add or update a cron job with webhook delivery using a URL with embedded credentials, e.g. https://user:pass@example.invalid/webhook. 2. Observe that the webhook URL is rejected (job will not use it). 3. Use the same job with https://example.invalid/webhook (no credentials) and confirm it is accepted. ### Expected - URLs with user:pass@ in the authority are rejected. - URLs without embedded credentials continue to work. ### Actual - Behavior matches above ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl returns null for URLs like http://user:pass@host/path; returns the URL for http://host/path. - Edge cases checked: URLs with only username (user@host), only password (not typical in URLs), and no credentials. - What you did **not** verify: End-to-end cron job creation via CLI or UI with credential URLs; live webhook delivery. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`No`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`Yes`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Update any cron job webhook URLs that include user:pass@ in the URL. Remove embedded credentials and use headers or query params for auth instead. ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. - Files/config to restore: src/cron/webhook-url.ts - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Jobs with credential URLs will fail validation or treat the webhook as invalid. ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Existing jobs using credential URLs will break. - Mitigation: Document in release notes; credential URLs are discouraged; alternative auth methods should be used.

The PR adds a loader-time plugin import policy, invokes it before plugin module evaluation, adds loader tests for allowed and blocked imports, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path for the main blockers: a plugin can declare `openclaw` and import `openclaw/runtime`, use computed specifiers, or hide imports in plugin-local dependency code while the static policy does not reject those paths.

target.title: Plugins: enforce private import boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins could couple directly to private OpenClaw internals or escape their package root through imports, even though the intended contract is the plugin SDK seam.
- Why it matters: That makes plugin behavior depend on unstable host internals and weakens the architecture boundary between plugin code and core.
- What changed: Added an enforced loader-time import policy that allows `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`, declared package dependencies, Node builtins, and in-package relative imports, while blocking private `openclaw/*` imports, undeclared bare packages, and imports that escape the plugin root.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not add process isolation or a sandbox; plugins still run arbitrary JS in-process.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Third-party and bundled plugins now fail to load when they import private OpenClaw package paths, undeclared bare packages, or files outside their own package root.
- Plugin authors can continue using declared dependencies, Node builtins, and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` seams.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Plugin code can no longer import private OpenClaw internals or escape its package root through the loader import path. This is contract hardening, not malicious-plugin sandboxing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader
- Relevant config (redacted): local temp plugin fixtures

### Steps

1. Create a plugin that imports `openclaw/runtime` or `../outside.cjs`.
2. Load it through the plugin loader.
3. Create a plugin that imports `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, a declared dependency, and an in-package helper.

### Expected

- Private OpenClaw imports and root-escaping imports are rejected before plugin execution.
- Declared dependencies, SDK seams, and in-package helpers continue to load.

### Actual

- Matches expected in direct local probes.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm build`
  - repo `pnpm check` pipeline via `scripts/committer`
  - direct `node --import tsx` probe rejecting `openclaw/runtime`
  - direct `node --import tsx` probe allowing declared dependency + local helper import
- Edge cases checked:
  - plugin root escape via relative import
  - declared dependency allowlist from plugin-local `package.json`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full `src/plugins/loader.test.ts` suite completion in this worktree
  - real third-party plugins yet

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Mostly yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) Maybe
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Plugin authors importing private OpenClaw internals must move to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` or declare/package their own dependencies locally.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Revert commit `6a8e3526fa`
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/plugins/import-policy.ts`
  - `src/plugins/loader.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - plugins failing at load with `plugin import policy violation`
  - plugins relying on undeclared dependencies or private `openclaw/*` imports

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - Existing plugins may rely on undeclared bare packages or private host imports and now fail fast.
  - Mitigation:
  - The failure is early and explicit, and the allowed path is narrow: declare the dependency or move to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`.
